relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: A straying animal becomes the neighbor's responsibility, not an excuse to look away.
What is lost is sheltered until relationship can restore it.
Garment, animal, and every lost thing must be returned.
A fallen animal and struggling neighbor must be raised together.
Ancient gendered clothing boundaries are stated within their own social world.
The nest places human appetite beside the future of a living species.
The reproducing mother is released before the young are taken.
A roof must be built so preventable death cannot fall from it.
The vineyard is guarded from a mixture treated as forfeiting its yield.
Unequal animals may not be forced beneath one plow.
Wool and linen remain separate in ordinary clothing.
Tassels place remembrance at the garment's edges.
A husband's hatred becomes the doorway to a sexual accusation.
He turns private contempt into public damage to her name.
Her parents must defend her within a system built around bodily proof.
The father names the husband's hatred before the elders.
A cloth is made to carry a burden no cloth can reliably prove.
A false accuser is publicly disciplined.
He is fined and forbidden to discard the woman he slandered.
If the accusation is accepted, the law turns against the young woman.
Patriarchal honor culture ends in her execution at her father's door.
Adultery is answered with death for both parties.
A betrothed woman encountered in a city is placed under a dangerous presumption.
Her silence is treated as consent, though trauma can silence a body.
In the field, force is recognized and only the attacker is condemned.
The survivor is declared innocent, and rape is compared to murder.
The absence of rescue explains why her cry could not save her.
An unbetrothed young woman is seized and violated.
The ancient remedy binds her future to the man who harmed her.
The chapter closes by prohibiting seizure of a father's wife.

contemplative_note: Deuteronomy 22 contains genuine neighbor care beside dangerous sexual and gender assumptions. Its modern ethical path is not hard to name: help what is lost, prevent avoidable harm, protect animals, believe survivors, honor consent, and refuse every use of scripture against trans people or assaulted women.
